what: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
text: Provides estimation plotting tools exposure-response models use logistic regression binary responses.

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
text: can install development version erlr like :

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
code: library(erlr) library(tibble)  lr_data #> # A tibble: 300 × 6 #>       id  dose exposure exposure_quartile response sex    #>    <int> <dbl>    <dbl> <fct>                <dbl> <fct>  #>  1     1   100    148.  Q3                       1 Male   #>  2     2   100     79.7 Q1                       1 Male   #>  3     3   200    212.  Q3                       1 Male   #>  4     4   200    236.  Q3                       0 Female #>  5     5     0      0   Placebo                  1 Female #>  6     6   200     71.0 Q1                       1 Male   #>  7     7   100    173.  Q3                       1 Male   #>  8     8   100    123.  Q2                       0 Female #>  9     9     0      0   Placebo                  0 Male   #> 10    10   200    165.  Q3                       1 Male   #> # ℹ 290 more rows  mod <- lr_model(response ~ exposure, lr_data) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),  #>     data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)     exposure   #>     0.15078      0.01112   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       341.7  #> Residual Deviance: 283.9     AIC: 287.9

title: Predictions and confidence intervals for logistic regression — lr_predict
code: mod <- lr_model(response ~ exposure, lr_data) lr_predict(mod, lr_data) #> # A tibble: 300 × 11 #>       id  dose exposure exposure_quartile response sex    fit_link se_link #>    <int> <dbl>    <dbl> <fct>                <dbl> <fct>     <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1     1   100    148.  Q3                       1 Male      1.80    0.248 #>  2     2   100     79.7 Q1                       1 Male      1.04    0.157 #>  3     3   200    212.  Q3                       1 Male      2.50    0.362 #>  4     4   200    236.  Q3                       0 Female    2.77    0.407 #>  5     5     0      0   Placebo                  1 Female    0.151   0.177 #>  6     6   200     71.0 Q1                       1 Male      0.940   0.151 #>  7     7   100    173.  Q3                       1 Male      2.08    0.292 #>  8     8   100    123.  Q2                       0 Female    1.52    0.209 #>  9     9     0      0   Placebo                  0 Male      0.151   0.177 #> 10    10   200    165.  Q3                       1 Male      1.99    0.277 #> # ℹ 290 more rows #> # ℹ 3 more variables: fit_resp <dbl>, ci_lower <dbl>, ci_upper <dbl>

what: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: Simulate logistic regression model

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: object logistic regression model

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: function arguments param, data, type. param argument vector coefficients data argument data frame tibble type argument string indicating type prediction generate (defaults "response") Takes fitted glm object input returns function evaluate underlying structural model user-specified parameters data (e.g., VPCs counterfactual simulation scenarios). principle work glms generally, merely logistic regressions, tested except logistic regression models

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
code: mod1 <- lr_model(response ~ exposure + sex, lr_data) par1 <- coef(mod1) mod1_sim <- lr_simulator(mod1)  # no counterfactuals p1 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = lr_data)  p2 <- unname(predict(mod1, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the data set lr_data2 <- lr_data[1:20, ] p3 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = lr_data2)  p4 <- unname(predict(mod1, newdata = lr_data2, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the parameters par2 <- par1 int1 <- par1["(Intercept)"] par2["(Intercept)"] <- 0 p5 <- mod1_sim(param = par2, data = lr_data)
